operating_state: NIKE revenues declined 8% to $12.4 billion in Q2 2025, driven by lower wholesale shipments, decreased traffic, and elevated promotional activity across NIKE Direct. Footwear revenues declined 12% due to lower unit sales and ASP reduction from higher discounts and channel mix changes. NIKE Direct revenues fell 13% with digital sales declining 21% and comparable store sales down 2%, while wholesale revenues decreased 3% as management repositions the business through inventory liquidation and marketplace rebalancing.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed 100 basis points to 43.6% primarily from higher discounts and unfavorable channel mix, partially offset by lower product input costs and reduced warehousing expenses. Lower NIKE Brand ASP decreased gross margin approximately 330 basis points while higher inventory obsolescence reserves added pressure, mitigated by 260 basis points improvement from lower product costs and 50 basis points benefit from reduced logistics expenses. Total selling and administrative expenses decreased 3% as lower operating overhead offset higher demand creation investments.
material_events: Elliott Hill assumed the role of Chief Executive Officer, leading strategic repositioning efforts including marketplace management actions and repositioning NIKE Brand Digital as a full-price platform. Nike repurchased 27.9 million shares for $2,254 million during the first six months at an average price of $80.83 per share, with dividend payments increasing to $1,115 million. Total endorsement contract obligations increased to $15.9 billion with $1.9 billion payable within 12 months due to contract renewals and additions.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation provided $127 million revenue benefit but created $23 million unfavorable impact on pre-tax income during Q2 2025. New U.S. Treasury foreign currency regulations effective June 2025 will require recognition of pre-transition foreign currency losses, with Nike expecting a one-time non-cash deferred tax benefit in Q3 2025.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued negative impact on revenues and gross margin over the next several quarters as repositioning actions continue, alongside higher demand creation expenses to support key product launches and sports marketing. Strategic priorities include leading with sport, building complete product portfolios, creating consumer connection stories, and repositioning digital platforms for full-price selling.
uncertainty_profile: The business transformation creates near-term revenue and margin pressure as management liquidates inventory through increased markdowns and elevated sales-related returns with wholesale partners. Inventory levels increased 6% to $8.0 billion, primarily driven by higher unit counts requiring continued clearance activities. Success of the repositioning strategy depends on consumer acceptance of new product innovations and effectiveness of marketplace rebalancing efforts.
